lenses.name: SRP (Single Responsibility Principle)
lenses.description: Each prompt file must do exactly one thing. A prompt violates SRP if it combines multiple distinct responsibilities (intake+validation, authoring+verification, routing+reporting, etc.) in a single role file.

criteria.test: Prompt declares or performs more than one distinct output concern — writes to 2+ unrelated output paths, has 2+ unrelated task-step sections, or its role name covers 2+ separable functions.
Prompt's task steps cross a phase boundary (e.g. a Build-phase prompt also does aPRD intake; an ADR prompt also writes HLD artifacts).
Prompt's role/lane description lists 2+ separable responsibilities without a clear single primary.
Prompt contains conditional branching that makes it behave as a different role depending on a flag — mode-switching within one file where the modes have different primary outputs.

findings.finding: Declares two unrelated output paths in frontmatter: `.audit/report.md` (operator-facing audit report) and `.aprd/00-raw-request.md` (Phase-0 ADP intake artifact for downstream pipeline ingestion). Different downstream consumers, different schemas, different phases. Two separable functions: produce audit report vs generate ADP intake artifact.
findings.remediation: Split into two roles: one writes `.audit/report.md` (audit presentation), one writes `.aprd/00-raw-request.md` (intake promotion). Or fold the intake artifact write into a separate AUDIT-PROMOTE role.

findings.finding: Role description states two co-equal outputs: 'writes `.audit/report.md` + `.aprd/00-raw-request.md`' — report writing and intake artifact generation listed without a single named primary responsibility.
findings.remediation: Clarify single primary (audit report) with intake artifact as byproduct; or split roles per LC1 remediation.

findings.finding: Declares three output paths across modes: `.build/skeleton/diagnosis.json`, `.build/slices/<id>/diagnosis.json` (build-red adjudication outputs), and `.aprd/diagnosis.json` (Phase-0 bugfix intake output). The third output is in `.aprd/` namespace and serves Phase-0 intake — fundamentally different from build-red self-heal-vs-escape adjudication. Two unrelated primary concerns in one file.
findings.remediation: Split BUGFIX-LOCALIZE (Part C) into a separate role in phase 00-aprd. Keep DIAGNOSE in 04-build for build-red adjudication only (Parts A + B).

findings.finding: Prompt declared in phase 04-build. Part C (BUGFIX-LOCALIZE) performs Phase-0 intake work: reads change-requests, reads frozen aprd.frozen.md, reproduces/localizes/root-causes a defect, writes `.aprd/diagnosis.json`. Phase-0 intake operations inside a Phase-4-labeled prompt = phase boundary crossing.
findings.remediation: Move BUGFIX-LOCALIZE to a separate prompt in 00-aprd phase. DIAGNOSE (04-build) handles only build-red adjudication.

findings.finding: Three modes where bugfix-localize behaves as a completely different role (Phase-0 intake: reproduce+localize+root-cause) vs build-red modes (build verification adjudication). Different primary outputs (`.aprd/diagnosis.json` vs `.build/*/diagnosis.json`) and different trigger signals.
findings.remediation: Extract BUGFIX-LOCALIZE into separate role. Remaining two modes (skeleton-build, slice-build) share same primary function and would no longer trigger LC4.

findings.finding: Declares two unrelated output paths: `.roadmap/05-sequence.json` (proposed sequencing artifact consumed by SKELETON-IDENTIFY/FOUNDATION-CUT/SEQUENCE-REVIEW) and `.roadmap/08-rerank.json` (living roadmap consumed by slice build loop and RE-RANK). Greenfield path writes 05-sequence.json only; feature-add path writes 08-rerank.json only. Two separable output concerns serving different downstream stages.
findings.remediation: Split feature-add sequence emission into a separate role, or restrict SEQUENCE to outputting only 05-sequence.json, with RE-RANK (or new SEQUENCE-FEATURE-ADD role) producing 08-rerank.json for the feature-add path.

findings.finding: Greenfield and feature-add branches produce different primary outputs (05-sequence.json vs 08-rerank.json) and omit different pipeline stages (no skeleton pin, no FOUNDATION-CUT consumption in feature-add). Two branches behave as different roles.
findings.remediation: Split into SEQUENCE (greenfield) and SEQUENCE-FEATURE-ADD roles, each with single primary output.

findings.finding: Skeleton pass declares two distinct unrelated output paths: `.hld/skeleton/test-specs.json` AND `.hld/skeleton/build-dag.json`. These serve unrelated concerns — test oracle specifications vs build dependency graph. Two distinct task-step sections with separate discriminators and separate consumers (MATERIALIZE-ORACLE uses test-specs; IMPLEMENT uses build-dag).
findings.remediation: Extract build-dag emission into a separate role (e.g. DERIVE-BUILD-DAG). DERIVE-TESTS outputs only test-specs.json.

findings.finding: Bugfix pass (Part C) behaves as a substantially different role: re-enters existing slice, mints reproduction test + regression layer instead of contract/flow/acceptance specs, triggered by class==bugfix+diagnosis.json. Different primary outputs (reproduction/regression layers vs standard test-specs) and different trigger signal from skeleton/increment modes.
findings.remediation: Extract bugfix pass into separate prompt (e.g. DERIVE-TESTS-BUGFIX). DERIVE-TESTS handles skeleton|increment passes only.

findings.finding: Greenfield branch produces draft (`.aprd/drafts/aprd.v1.md`) requiring subsequent CRITIQUE + sign-off gate. Feature-add and bugfix branches produce frozen version directly plus re-sign `aprd.lock`. Different primary outputs (draft vs immediately-frozen artifact + lock re-sign) and different downstream consequences. Greenfield never writes `aprd.lock`; feature-add/bugfix always write it.
findings.remediation: Consider splitting greenfield SYNTHESIZE (draft awaiting CRITIQUE) from SYNTHESIZE-INCREMENT (frozen version bump + lock re-sign). Alternatively accept mode branching as designed given single conceptual function (produce aPRD contract for current class).

findings.finding: Bugfix branch replaces component-build with minimal in-place edit of existing code at BLAST_RADIUS symbol — opposite behavior to greenfield/feature-add (write new component vs edit existing symbol). Different dispatch signal (diagnosis.json + class==bugfix vs build-plan dispatch). Different primary behavioral mode within one file.
findings.remediation: Consider extracting bugfix repair into separate IMPLEMENT-BUGFIX role. Low priority — outputs remain in same namespace.

findings.finding: Bugfix branch materializes reproduction+regression layers instead of full contract/flow/acceptance oracle; supersedes existing slice oracle (in-place re-entry). No build-plan dispatch. Substantially different behavior from greenfield/feature-add modes.
findings.remediation: Consider extracting bugfix oracle materialization into separate role. Low priority — same output namespace.

findings.finding: Bugfix mode runs different verification bar (reproduction red→green + scoped regression) instead of full contract/flow/acceptance/class-ext ladder. Different dispatch signal (oracle_layers:[reproduction,regression]). Behaves as different verification function for bugfix class.
findings.remediation: Consider extracting bugfix verification into separate role. Low priority — same output namespace.
